missables: Rod of Wisdom — Destruction Sphere, Besaid Temple. Dark Valefor blocks return in HD.
sgTip: Yuna joins here. Prioritise NulBlaze and NulShock — they trivialise the next two Sinspawn fights.

subLocations.name: Beach
subLocations.prose: After Wakka shoves Tidus into the lagoon, dive to the southeast wall for three sunken chests — two Antidotes and 200 Gil. Back on the beach, the Moon Crest is in a small cove just east of where you met the Aurochs. Don't leave the island without it — it's missable once you sail away.

subLocations.name: Besaid Village
subLocations.prose: Sweep the village before resting — chests near the first tent and outside the Crusaders Lodge hold Phoenix Down, two Potions, a Hi-Potion, and 400 Gil. Stop by the Crusaders Lodge to speak to Luzzu and Gatta. After the campfire scene, rest at the inn; the next morning Wakka gives you Brotherhood and Yuna joins. Al Bhed Primer Vol. II is on the ground near the lodge's reception desk — grab it before leaving.

subLocations.name: Besaid Temple
subLocations.prose: Work through the Cloister and place the Besaid Sphere in the pedestal to expose the Destruction Sphere slot. Before you push the pedestal to the final room, detour: place the Destruction Sphere in the slot where you found the Besaid Sphere to reveal a hidden chamber with the Rod of Wisdom. If you skip this step there's no going back — Dark Valefor blocks the temple entrance in the HD version once you have the airship.

bosses.strategy: Valefor is obtained here, not fought as an enemy. No strategy needed — this entry exists for tracking purposes.

Tidus's first mandatory 1-on-1 fight (750 HP, Overkill at 300). Kimahri alternates between a standard physical attack and a Jump attack — neither hits especially hard at this stage. There are no elemental weaknesses to exploit and nothing to steal. Use Cheer at the start to reduce incoming damage from both attacks, then just keep swinging. Don't waste Potions — Tidus's HP and MP are fully restored after the fight regardless of how low they drop. If Tidus's Overdrive gauge is charged, use Spiral Cut to end things quickly. This fight is intentionally winnable even if you just attack every turn.
